 # Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype
 
 This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.
 
 ## Tasks
 
-- [ ] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
+- [x] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
   - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
   - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
   - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules
+  - Note: baseline mapping and reuse-first target slice captured on 2026-04-30 in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md`
 
 - [ ] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
   - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
   - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
   - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
   - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status
 
 - [ ] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
   - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
   - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work
   - Ensure the prototype allows at minimum: open session, refresh session state, inspect current status, and navigate back to the existing agent-chat or cloud-agent experience
   - Keep the UI intentionally simple but visually compelling so the user can see live orchestration progress as soon as the phase is complete
 
 - [ ] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
   - Reuse existing event emitters, output channels, and telemetry helpers before adding new instrumentation paths
   - Make tree updates and orchestration view updates respond to session-store, registry, and cloud-provider changes without manual reloads where possible
   - Add actionable empty, loading, and degraded states that explain whether there are no sessions, missing providers, or failed status reads
   - Write implementation notes in `docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md` with YAML front matter and wiki-links to `[[Running-Agents-Prototype]]`
 
 - [ ] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
   - Add or update unit tests for the normalized orchestration read model, lifecycle grouping, and any new state selectors or adapters
   - Add webview or provider-focused tests that verify the prototype renders meaningful empty, active, and failed states from representative session data
   - Prefer extending existing test suites around providers and stores before creating entirely new harnesses
 
 - [ ] Run the orchestration quality gates and fix failures until the prototype is working end-to-end:
   - Run the relevant targeted tests for orchestration changes first
   - Run `npm run check`
   - Run the full affected test command if targeted tests expose regressions that require broader validation
   - Verify the extension can surface the new orchestration prototype without breaking the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents views

 # Running Agents Prototype
 
 See also: [[Running-Agents-Model]], [[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]
 
-## Goal
+## Baseline Mapping
 
-Deliver the smallest viable orchestration surface by composing the existing Agent Chat and Cloud Agents runtime state into one read model and one webview.
+The current orchestration surface already exists in pieces, and the prototype should compose those pieces instead of starting a parallel stack.
 
+- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` already groups local agent-chat sessions into `Active`, `Recent`, and `Orphaned worktrees`, listens to both store and registry change events, and routes session clicks into `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`.
+- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` is already the canonical in-extension detail surface for local and restored agent-chat sessions, pushes session lists to the webview, and reuses the shared page registry plus `getWebviewContent()` bootstrap path.
+- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` already projects stored cloud sessions into a provider-aware tree, including status icons, task children, PR links, and provider context keys.
+- `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts` already handles one session-bound editor panel for agent chat, but its transport and hydration patterns are narrower than the sidebar view provider and should not become a second orchestration root.
+- `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts` already serializes cloud session progress into a webview payload and supports refresh plus external-link actions.
+- `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx` already defines the empty, loading, error, and populated rendering states the orchestration prototype can mirror instead of inventing a separate state vocabulary.
+
+## Smallest Viable Prototype
+
+The smallest viable prototype is one normalized read model and one orchestration webview that sit on top of existing stores, registries, and page wiring.
+
+- Reuse `AgentChatSessionStore` plus `AgentChatRegistry` as the local-session authority.
+- Reuse `AgentSessionStorage` plus `ProviderRegistry` as the cloud-session authority.
+- Reuse existing change emitters from those services to refresh both the tree surfaces and the orchestration surface from the same underlying state changes.
+- Reuse the shared page registry and `getWebviewContent()` instead of introducing a second routing system or standalone webview bootstrap path.
+- Reuse existing open-session commands so orchestration cards navigate back to `agent-chat` or the cloud-provider surface rather than replacing them.
+
 ## Reused Modules
 
-- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` remains the tree-first summary for agent-chat sessions.
-- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` remains the canonical session-detail surface for local and bridged chat sessions.
-- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` remains the cloud tree surface.
-- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts` and `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts` remain the source of truth for local session lifecycle.
-- `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`, `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`, and `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service.ts` remain the source of truth for cloud progress.
-- `ui/src/page-registry.tsx` and `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts` remain the shared page-registration and webview bootstrap path.
+- `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`
+- `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`
+- `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`
+- `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`
+- `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`
+- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts`
+- `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts`
+- `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`
+- `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`
+- `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
+- `ui/src/page-registry.tsx`
+- `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts`
 
 ## New Modules
 
-- `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` provides a normalized projection for local and cloud sessions.
-- `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` owns the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
-- `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` renders the prototype dashboard.
+- `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` for the merged projection only.
+- `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` for the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
+- `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` for the orchestration dashboard surface.
 
-## Prototype Slice
+## Target Slice
 
-- One read model merges agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions.
-- One webview presents four buckets: Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed.
-- Each card supports open session, refresh state, inspect lifecycle metadata, and jump back to the existing surface.
-- Existing trees stay intact and continue to update independently.
+- Merge local agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions into one projection with shared lifecycle buckets.
+- Render four visible groups: `Active`, `Waiting`, `Completed`, and `Failed`.
+- Surface current metadata that already exists or is trivially derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status.
+- Keep the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents trees intact while the orchestration view becomes an aggregated read-only overview.
+- Route actions back into existing session-opening and refresh flows instead of creating orchestration-specific mutations.
 
 ## Constraints
 
 - No second orchestration state store.
 - No new routing layer.
-- No mutation path inside the read model beyond delegating to current commands.
-- Cloud sessions open externally when there is no richer in-extension detail surface yet.
+- No duplicate session lifecycle model.
+- No orchestration-owned mutation path beyond delegating to existing commands.
+- Cloud sessions should continue to open in their current best surface, including external URLs where that is the only richer detail view today.

"# Context

Your name is **Dori**, a Maestro-managed AI agent. You are executing tasks from a **Playbook** — a collection of Auto Run documents. Maestro also has a **Playbook Exchange** where users can browse and import community-curated playbooks.

- **Agent Path:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
- **Git Branch:** 018-agent-chat-panel
- **Auto Run Folder:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs
- **Loop Iteration:** 00001
- **Working Folder for Temporary Files:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Working

If you need to create the working folder, do so.

---

## CRITICAL: Response Format Requirement

**Your response MUST begin with a specific, actionable synopsis of what you accomplished.**

- GOOD examples: \"Added pagination to the user list component\", \"Fixed authentication timeout bug in login.ts\", \"Refactored database queries to use prepared statements\"
- BAD examples: \"The task is complete\", \"Task completed successfully\", \"Done\", \"Finished the task\"

The synopsis is displayed in the History panel and must describe the actual work done, not just that work was done.

---

## Structured Output Artifacts

When creating documentation, research notes, reports, or any knowledge artifacts (not source code), use **structured Markdown** by default:

### YAML Front Matter

```yaml
---
type: research | note | report | analysis | reference
title: Descriptive Title
created: YYYY-MM-DD
tags:
  - relevant-tag
related:
  - '[[Other-Document]]'
---
```

### Wiki-Link Cross-References

Use `[[Document-Name]]` syntax to connect related documents. This enables graph exploration in Maestro's DocGraph viewer and tools like Obsidian.

### Folder Organization

Organize artifacts in logical folders by entity type or domain:

```
docs/
├── research/
│   ├── topic-a.md
│   └── topic-b.md
├── architecture/
│   └── system-design.md
└── decisions/
    └── adr-001-choice.md
```

**When to apply:** Research findings, competitive analysis, architecture decisions, technical specs, meeting notes, reference docs, glossaries.

**When NOT to apply:** Source code files, config files (JSON/YAML), generated assets, temporary files.

## Instructions

1. Project Orientation
   Begin by reviewing CLAUDE.md (when available) in this folder to understand the project's structure, conventions, and workflow expectations.

2. Task Selection
   Process the FIRST unchecked task (- [ ]) from top to bottom. Note that there may be relevant images associated with the task. If there are, analyze them, and include in your final synopsis back how many images you analyzed in preparation for solving the task.

   IMPORTANT: You will only work on this single task. If the task description contains multiple steps or subtasks (e.g., bullet points, numbered lists, or semicolon-separated items), treat them as one cohesive unit—they were intentionally grouped by the document author to share context. Complete all parts before marking done. Do not move on to the next major checkbox item.

3. Task Evaluation
   - Fully understand the task and inspect the relevant code.
   - Identify all subtasks within the current checkbox item.
   - There will be future runs to take care of other checkbox items.

4. Task Implementation
   - **Before creating new code**, search for existing implementations, utilities, helpers, or patterns in the codebase that can be reused or extended. Avoid duplicating functionality that already exists.
   - Implement the task according to the project's established style, architecture, and coding norms.
   - Ensure that test cases are created, and that they pass.
   - Ensure you haven't broken any existing test cases.

5. Completion + Reporting
   - Mark the task as completed by changing \"- [ ]\" to \"- [x]\".
   - Begin your response with the specific synopsis (see \"Response Format Requirement\" above).
   - Follow with any relevant details about:
     - Implementation approach or key decisions made
     - Why the task was intentionally skipped (if applicable)
     - If implementation failed, explain the failure and do NOT check off the item.

6. Version Control
   For any code or documentation changes, if we're in a Github repo:
   - Commit using a descriptive message prefixed with \"MAESTRO: \".
   - Push to GitHub.
   - Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, or any other top-level documentation if appropriate.

7. Exit Immediately
   After completing (or skipping) your task, EXIT. Do not proceed to additional tasks—another agent instance will handle them. If there are no remaining open tasks, exit immediately and state that there is nothing left to do.

---

## Tasks

Process tasks from this document:

/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Initiation/2026-04-30-gatomia-orchestration-playbook/GATOMIA-ORCHESTRATION-01.md

Check off tasks and add any relevant notes around the completion directly within that document.
"
Reviewing the playbook task and project guidance first, then I’ll implement only the first open checkbox item and verify it with tests and `npm run check`.

1: /**
2:  * RunningAgentsTreeProvider — "Running Agents" tree view (spec 018, T044).
3:  *
4:  * Renders three groups at the root level:
5:  *
6:  *   1. **Active**             — sessions with a non-terminal lifecycle state.
7:  *   2. **Recent**              — sessions in a terminal lifecycle state.
8:  *   3. **Orphaned worktrees**  — worktree handles whose owner session has
9:  *                                been evicted by retention (§2.4 of the
10:  *                                storage contract). Populated by T075.
11:  *
12:  * Each session leaf carries a click command targeting
13:  * `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession` (wired by T038) and a context value
14:  * that enables right-click menu entries (e.g. "Clean up worktree" for
15:  * worktree-backed sessions, T048).
16:  *
17:  * The provider refreshes whenever either the session store's manifest or
18:  * the in-memory registry emits a change event — so tree updates are
19:  * driven from whichever authority mutated first.
20:  *
21:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/tasks.md §T042 / §T044
22:  */
23: 
24: import {
25: 	type Event,
26: 	EventEmitter,
27: 	ThemeIcon,
28: 	TreeItem,
29: 	TreeItemCollapsibleState,
30: 	type TreeDataProvider,
31: } from "vscode";
32: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
33: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";
34: import type {
35: 	AgentChatSession,
36: 	OrphanedWorktreeEntry,
37: 	SessionManifest,
38: } from "../features/agent-chat/types";
39: 
40: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: // Public shape
42: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43: 
44: export type RunningAgentsTreeItem = TreeItem & {
45: 	/** Present on session leaf nodes; absent on group and orphan nodes. */
46: 	sessionId?: string;
47: 	/** Present on orphan leaves; absent otherwise. */
48: 	orphanId?: string;
49: 	/** Present on orphan leaves so the right-click handler can build a cleanup payload. */
50: 	orphanAbsolutePath?: string;
51: 	/** Present on orphan leaves. */
52: 	orphanBranchName?: string;
53: 	/** Discriminator for getChildren dispatch. */
54: 	nodeKind:
55: 		| "new-session-leaf"
56: 		| "group-active"
57: 		| "group-recent"
58: 		| "group-orphans"
59: 		| "session"
60: 		| "orphan";
61: };
62: 
63: export interface RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions {
64: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
65: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Event fired whenever the store's manifest changes. Injected so tests
68: 	 * can drive refreshes without reaching into `store.onDidChangeManifest`
69: 	 * private implementation details.
70: 	 */
71: 	readonly storeChangeEvent: Event<SessionManifest>;
72: }
73: 
74: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75: // Constants
76: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77: 
78: const OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.openForSession";
79: const NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID = "gatomia.agentChat.newSession";
80: const NEW_SESSION_LABEL = "+ New agent session…";
81: 
82: const GROUP_LABELS = {
83: 	active: "Active",
84: 	recent: "Recent",
85: 	orphans: "Orphaned worktrees",
86: } as const;
87: 
88: const GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES = {
89: 	active: "agent-chat-group-active",
90: 	recent: "agent-chat-group-recent",
91: 	orphans: "agent-chat-group-orphans",
92: } as const;
93: 
94: const SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session";
95: const SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-session-worktree";
96: const ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE = "agent-chat-orphan-worktree";
97: 
98: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99: // Provider
100: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101: 
102: export class RunningAgentsTreeProvider
103: 	implements TreeDataProvider<RunningAgentsTreeItem>
104: {
105: 	static readonly viewId = "gatomia.views.runningAgents";
106: 
107: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
108: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
109: 	>();
110: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<
111: 		RunningAgentsTreeItem | undefined | null | void
112: 	> = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
113: 
114: 	private readonly disposables: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
115: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
116: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
117: 
118: 	constructor(options: RunningAgentsTreeProviderOptions) {
119: 		this.store = options.store;
120: 		this.registry = options.registry;
121: 
122: 		this.disposables.push(
123: 			options.storeChangeEvent(() => {
124: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
125: 			})
126: 		);
127: 		this.disposables.push(
128: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
129: 				this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
130: 			})
131: 		);
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	dispose(): void {
135: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
136: 			try {
137: 				d.dispose();
138: 			} catch {
139: 				// best-effort
140: 			}
141: 		}
142: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
143: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	refresh(): void {
147: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire();
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	getTreeItem(element: RunningAgentsTreeItem): TreeItem {
151: 		return element;
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	getChildren(
155: 		element?: RunningAgentsTreeItem
156: 	): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
157: 		if (!element) {
158: 			return Promise.resolve([
159: 				this.newSessionLeaf(),
160: 				this.groupItem("active"),
161: 				this.groupItem("recent"),
162: 				this.groupItem("orphans"),
163: 			]);
164: 		}
165: 
166: 		switch (element.nodeKind) {
167: 			case "group-active":
168: 				return this.renderActiveLeaves();
169: 			case "group-recent":
170: 				return this.renderRecentLeaves();
171: 			case "group-orphans":
172: 				return this.renderOrphanLeaves();
173: 			default:
174: 				return Promise.resolve([]);
175: 		}
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	private newSessionLeaf(): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
179: 		const item = new TreeItem(
180: 			NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
181: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
182: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
183: 		item.nodeKind = "new-session-leaf";
184: 		item.contextValue = "agent-chat-new-session";
185: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("add");
186: 		item.command = {
187: 			command: NEW_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
188: 			title: NEW_SESSION_LABEL,
189: 		};
190: 		item.tooltip = "Start a new agent chat session";
191: 		return item;
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
195: 	// Group + leaf factories
196: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
197: 
198: 	private groupItem(
199: 		kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"
200: 	): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
201: 		const item = new TreeItem(
202: 			GROUP_LABELS[kind],
203: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded
204: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
205: 		item.contextValue = GROUP_CONTEXT_VALUES[kind];
206: 		item.nodeKind = `group-${kind}` as RunningAgentsTreeItem["nodeKind"];
207: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(groupIcon(kind));
208: 		return item;
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	private async renderActiveLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
212: 		const sessions = await this.store.listActive();
213: 		return sessions
214: 			.slice()
215: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
216: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
217: 	}
218: 
219: 	private async renderRecentLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
220: 		const sessions = await this.store.listRecent();
221: 		return sessions
222: 			.slice()
223: 			.sort(sortByUpdatedAtDesc)
224: 			.map((s) => this.sessionLeaf(s));
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	private async renderOrphanLeaves(): Promise<RunningAgentsTreeItem[]> {
228: 		const orphans = await this.store.listOrphanedWorktrees();
229: 		return orphans
230: 			.slice()
231: 			.sort((a, b) => b.recordedAt - a.recordedAt)
232: 			.map((o) => this.orphanLeaf(o));
233: 	}
234: 
235: 	private sessionLeaf(session: AgentChatSession): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
236: 		const item = new TreeItem(
237: 			session.agentDisplayName,
238: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
239: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
240: 		item.sessionId = session.id;
241: 		item.nodeKind = "session";
242: 		item.description = describeSession(session);
243: 		item.tooltip = `${session.agentDisplayName}${
244: 			session.selectedModeId ? ` · ${session.selectedModeId}` : ""
245: 		} · ${session.executionTarget.kind} · ${session.lifecycleState}`;
246: 		item.contextValue = isWorktreeBacked(session)
247: 			? SESSION_WORKTREE_CONTEXT_VALUE
248: 			: SESSION_CONTEXT_VALUE;
249: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon(lifecycleIcon(session.lifecycleState));
250: 		item.command = {
251: 			command: OPEN_FOR_SESSION_COMMAND_ID,
252: 			title: "Open Agent Chat",
253: 			arguments: [session.id],
254: 		};
255: 		return item;
256: 	}
257: 
258: 	private orphanLeaf(entry: OrphanedWorktreeEntry): RunningAgentsTreeItem {
259: 		const item = new TreeItem(
260: 			entry.branchName,
261: 			TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
262: 		) as RunningAgentsTreeItem;
263: 		item.orphanId = entry.sessionId;
264: 		item.orphanAbsolutePath = entry.absolutePath;
265: 		item.orphanBranchName = entry.branchName;
266: 		item.nodeKind = "orphan";
267: 		item.description = entry.absolutePath;
268: 		item.tooltip = `Orphaned worktree at ${entry.absolutePath} (branch ${entry.branchName})`;
269: 		item.contextValue = ORPHAN_CONTEXT_VALUE;
270: 		item.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-commit");
271: 		return item;
272: 	}
273: }
274: 
275: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
276: // Pure helpers
277: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
278: 
279: function sortByUpdatedAtDesc(a: AgentChatSession, b: AgentChatSession): number {
280: 	return b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt;
281: }
282: 
283: function isWorktreeBacked(session: AgentChatSession): boolean {
284: 	return session.executionTarget.kind === "worktree";
285: }
286: 
287: function describeSession(session: AgentChatSession): string {
288: 	const parts: string[] = [];
289: 	if (session.selectedModeId) {
290: 		parts.push(session.selectedModeId);
291: 	}
292: 	parts.push(session.executionTarget.kind);
293: 	parts.push(session.lifecycleState);
294: 	// T071 — surface the "blocked" flag for sessions waiting on the user so
295: 	// they don't get lost among Active entries.
296: 	if (session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input") {
297: 		parts.push("(blocked)");
298: 	}
299: 	return parts.join(" · ");
300: }
301: 
302: function groupIcon(kind: "active" | "recent" | "orphans"): string {
303: 	if (kind === "active") {
304: 		return "play-circle";
305: 	}
306: 	if (kind === "recent") {
307: 		return "history";
308: 	}
309: 	return "warning";
310: }
311: 
312: function lifecycleIcon(state: AgentChatSession["lifecycleState"]): string {
313: 	switch (state) {
314: 		case "initializing":
315: 			return "loading~spin";
316: 		case "running":
317: 			return "play";
318: 		case "waiting-for-input":
319: 			return "bell";
320: 		case "completed":
321: 			return "check";
322: 		case "failed":
323: 			return "error";
324: 		case "cancelled":
325: 			return "stop-circle";
326: 		case "ended-by-shutdown":
327: 			return "debug-disconnect";
328: 		default:
329: 			return "circle-outline";
330: 	}
331: }

(End of file - total 331 lines)

/**
 * RunningAgentsTreeProvider — "Running Agents" tree view (spec 018, T044).
 *
 * Renders three groups at the root level:
 *
 *   1. **Active**             — sessions with a non-terminal lifecycle state.
 *   2. **Recent**              — sessions in a terminal lifecycle state.
 *   3. **Orphaned worktrees**  — worktree handles whose owner session has
 *                                been evicted by retention (§2.4 of the
 *                                storage contract). Populated by T075.
 *
 * Each session leaf carries a click command targeting
 * `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession` (wired by T038) and a context value
 * that enables right-click menu entries (e.g. "Clean up worktree" for
 * worktree-backed sessions, T048).
 *
 * The provider refreshes whenever either the session store's manifest or
 * the in-memory registry emits a change event — so tree updates are
 * driven from whichever authority mutated first.
 *

297: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
298: 		// The chat view is declared twice in `package.json` (once in the
299: 		// auxiliary `gatomia-chat` container, once in the primary
300: 		// `gatomia` container) and gated with the
301: 		// `gatomia.host.chatInPrimary` context key — see the comment on
302: 		// `AGENT_CHAT_PRIMARY_VIEW_TYPE` for the rationale. Only one is
303: 		// ever visible at a time, but we don't track which one here, so
304: 		// we issue both `.focus` commands and let the inactive one
305: 		// no-op. The active one resolves the webview if it hasn't been
306: 		// resolved yet.
307: 		const candidateViewIds = [
308: 			AgentChatViewProvider.viewType,
309: 			AGENT_CHAT_PRIMARY_VIEW_TYPE,
310: 		];
311: 		// Issue both focus calls in parallel — the host renders only
312: 		// one of the two views (the other's `when` clause is false), so
313: 		// one call resolves and the other rejects/no-ops. Running them
314: 		// in parallel keeps `reveal()` consuming a single microtask
315: 		// roundtrip, which matters for callers that race subsequent
316: 		// `postMessage`s after the await (e.g. `startNewSessionFlow`).
317: 		await Promise.all(
318: 			candidateViewIds.map((viewId) =>
319: 				Promise.resolve(commands.executeCommand(`${viewId}.focus`)).catch(
320: 					() => {
321: 						// `<viewId>.focus` rejects when the view's `when`
322: 						// clause evaluates to false (host gated it out).
323: 						// The other id will succeed.
324: 					}
325: 				)
326: 			)
327: 		);
328: 		// If the view exists, also bring it forward (no-op when it isn't
329: 		// resolved yet — the workbench command above will resolve it).
330: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
331: 	}
332: 
333: 	async focusSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
334: 		await this.reveal();
335: 		if (!this.view) {
336: 			// View was not resolved yet — `resolveWebviewView` will pick up
337: 			// the pending id once it runs.
338: 			this.pendingFocusSessionId = sessionId;
339: 			return;
340: 		}
341: 		await this.bindSession(sessionId);
342: 	}
343: 
344: 	async startNewSessionFlow(): Promise<void> {
345: 		await this.reveal();
346: 		this.binding?.dispose();
347: 		this.binding = undefined;
348: 		await this.postMessage({
349: 			type: "agent-chat/session/cleared",
350: 			payload: { reason: "new-session-requested" },
351: 		});
352: 		this.pushSessionList();
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	pushCatalog(): void {
356: 		if (!this.view) {
357: 			return;
358: 		}
359: 		const catalog = this.snapshotCatalog();
360: 		this.postMessage({
361: 			type: "agent-chat/catalog/loaded",
362: 			payload: {
363: 				catalog,
364: 				modelsLoading: this.snapshotModelsLoading(),
365: 			},
366: 		}).catch(noop);
367: 		// Kick off (or piggy-back on) an async probe refresh so the next
368: 		// rebroadcast can replace the optimistic source-based heuristic
369: 		// with the real `descriptor.probe()` result.
370: 		this.scheduleProbeRefresh();
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	/**
374: 	 * Build the per-provider `modelsLoading` map shipped with every
375: 	 * `agent-chat/catalog/loaded` payload so the webview can render a
376: 	 * loading placeholder while a probe is in flight.
377: 	 */
378: 	private snapshotModelsLoading(): Record<string, boolean> {
379: 		const out: Record<string, boolean> = {};
380: 		for (const [providerId, loading] of this.modelsLoading) {
381: 			if (loading) {
382: 				out[providerId] = true;
383: 			}
384: 		}
385: 		return out;
386: 	}
387: 
388: 	pushSessionList(): void {
389: 		if (!this.view) {
390: 			return;
391: 		}
392: 		this.postMessage({
393: 			type: "agent-chat/sessions/list-changed",
394: 			payload: { sessions: this.snapshotSessionList() },
395: 		}).catch(noop);
396: 	}
397: 
398: 	pushPermissionDefault(): void {
399: 		if (!this.view) {
400: 			return;
401: 		}
402: 		this.postMessage({
403: 			type: "agent-chat/permission-default/changed",
404: 			payload: { mode: readPermissionDefaultFromConfig() },
405: 		}).catch(noop);
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	dispose(): void {
409: 		this.binding?.dispose();
410: 		this.binding = undefined;
411: 		for (const d of this.disposables) {
412: 			try {
413: 				d.dispose();
414: 			} catch {
415: 				// best-effort
416: 			}
417: 		}
418: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
419: 	}
420: 
421: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
422: 	// Inbound webview message routing
423: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
424: 
425: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
426: 		const msg = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
427: 		if (!msg?.type) {
428: 			return;
429: 		}
430: 		switch (msg.type) {
431: 			case "agent-chat/ready":
432: 				this.pushCatalog();
433: 				this.pushSessionList();
434: 				this.pushPermissionDefault();
435: 				if (this.binding) {
436: 					await this.binding.sendSessionLoaded();
437: 				} else {
438: 					await this.postMessage({
439: 						type: "agent-chat/session/cleared",
440: 						payload: { reason: "ready-no-binding" },
441: 					});
442: 				}
443: 				return;
444: 			case "agent-chat/control/switch-session": {
445: 				const sessionId = (msg.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined)
446: 					?.sessionId;
447: 				if (sessionId) {
448: 					await this.bindSession(sessionId);
449: 				}
450: 				return;
451: 			}
452: 			case "agent-chat/control/new-session": {
453: 				await this.dispatchNewSessionFromComposer(
454: 					msg.payload as NewSessionRequestPayload | undefined
455: 				);
456: 				return;
457: 			}
458: 			case "agent-chat/control/request-new-chat": {
459: 				await this.startNewSessionFlow();
460: 				return;
461: 			}
462: 			case "agent-chat/control/change-permission-default": {
463: 				await this.handleChangePermissionDefault(
464: 					msg.payload as { mode?: string } | undefined
465: 				);
466: 				return;
467: 			}
468: 			case "agent-chat/control/probe-models": {
469: 				const providerId = (msg.payload as { providerId?: string } | undefined)
470: 					?.providerId;
471: 				if (providerId) {
472: 					this.triggerModelProbe(providerId, { invalidate: true });
473: 				}
474: 				return;
475: 			}
476: 			default:
477: 				if (this.binding) {
478: 					await this.binding.handleWebviewMessage(msg);
479: 				}
480: 				return;
481: 		}
482: 	}
483: 
484: 	/**
485: 	 * Kick off a {@link ModelDiscoveryService.getModels} probe for a
486: 	 * provider, marking the per-provider loading state and rebroadcasting
487: 	 * the catalog so the webview can render the placeholder while the
488: 	 * probe is in flight. When `invalidate` is true the cached entry is
489: 	 * discarded first so the user-facing refresh button always re-queries
490: 	 * the agent.
491: 	 */
492: 	private triggerModelProbe(
493: 		providerId: string,
494: 		opts: { invalidate?: boolean } = {}
495: 	): void {
496: 		const discovery = this.options.modelDiscovery;
497: 		if (!discovery) {
498: 			return;
499: 		}
500: 		if (opts.invalidate) {
501: 			discovery.invalidate(providerId);
502: 		}
503: 		this.modelsLoading.set(providerId, true);
504: 		this.pushCatalog();
505: 		discovery
506: 			.getModels(providerId)
507: 			.catch((err: unknown) => {
508: 				this.options.outputChannel?.appendLine(
509: 					`[AgentChatView] model probe failed for ${providerId}: ${
510: 						err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
511: 					}`
512: 				);
513: 			})
514: 			.finally(() => {
515: 				this.modelsLoading.delete(providerId);
516: 				// `onDidChange` already rebroadcasts the catalog on success;
517: 				// we only force a push here to clear the loading marker
518: 				// when the probe rejects without firing a change event.
519: 				if (this.view) {
520: 					this.pushCatalog();
521: 				}
522: 			});
523: 	}
524: 
525: 	/**
526: 	 * Persists the requested permission default to the global VS Code
527: 	 * configuration. The change-of-config listener registered above will
528: 	 * pick up the update and rebroadcast `permission-default/changed`,
529: 	 * keeping the webview in sync without a manual round-trip from here.
530: 	 *
531: 	 * Unknown / malformed values are ignored — the webview remains
532: 	 * authoritative for its own UX state.
533: 	 */
534: 	private async handleChangePermissionDefault(
535: 		payload: { mode?: string } | undefined
536: 	): Promise<void> {
537: 		const mode = payload?.mode;
538: 		if (mode !== "ask" && mode !== "allow" && mode !== "deny") {
539: 			return;
540: 		}
541: 		try {
542: 			await workspace
543: 				.getConfiguration("gatomia")
544: 				.update("acp.permissionDefault", mode, ConfigurationTarget.Global);
545: 		} catch (err) {
546: 			this.options.outputChannel?.appendLine(
547: 				`[AgentChatView] permissionDefault update failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
548: 			);
549: 		}
550: 	}
551: 
552: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
553: 	// Binding
554: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
555: 
556: 	private async bindSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
557: 		if (this.binding && this.binding.sessionId === sessionId) {
558: 			await this.binding.sendSessionLoaded();
559: 			return;
560: 		}
561: 		this.binding?.dispose();
562: 		this.binding = undefined;
563: 
564: 		const session =
565: 			this.options.registry.getSession(sessionId) ??
566: 			(await this.options.store.getSession(sessionId));
567: 		if (!session) {
568: 			await this.postMessage({
569: 				type: "agent-chat/session/cleared",
570: 				payload: { reason: "session-not-found" },
571: 			});
572: 			return;
573: 		}
574: 		// Make sure the registry knows about the session so cancel/runner
575: 		// lookups still work after a reload.
576: 		if (!this.options.registry.getSession(sessionId)) {
577: 			this.options.registry.registerSession(session);
578: 		}
579: 
580: 		this.binding = new SidebarSessionBinding({
581: 			session,
582: 			store: this.options.store,
583: 			registry: this.options.registry,
584: 			postMessage: (m) => this.postMessage(m),
585: 			outputChannel: this.options.outputChannel,
586: 		});
587: 		await this.binding.sendSessionLoaded();
588: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
589: 			sessionId: session.id,
590: 			agentId: session.agentId,
591: 			surface: "sidebar",
592: 		});
593: 	}
594: 
595: 	private async dispatchNewSessionFromComposer(
596: 		payload: NewSessionRequestPayload | undefined
597: 	): Promise<void> {
598: 		if (!payload) {
599: 			return;
600: 		}
601: 		const trimmed = (payload.taskInstruction ?? "").trim();
602: 		if (trimmed.length === 0) {
603: 			window.showWarningMessage(
604: 				"Type a message before starting a new agent chat session."
605: 			);
606: 			return;
607: 		}
608: 		const provider = this.findProvider(payload.providerId);
609: 		if (!provider) {
610: 			window.showErrorMessage(
611: 				"Pick a provider before starting a new agent chat session."
612: 			);
613: 			return;
614: 		}
615: 		try {
616: 			await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.startNew", {
617: 				agentId: provider.id,
618: 				agentDisplayName: provider.displayName,
619: 				agentCommand: "",
620: 				mode: payload.modelId,
621: 				thinkingLevelId: payload.thinkingLevelId,
622: 				agentRoleId: payload.agentRoleId,
623: 				taskInstruction: composePromptWithAgentFile(
624: 					trimmed,
625: 					payload.agentFileId,
626: 					this.snapshotCatalog().agentFiles
627: 				),
628: 			});
629: 			// `startNew` will register the new session and the registry
630: 			// onDidChange listener will rebroadcast the session list. The
631: 			// view binds to the newest session (last by createdAt) below.
632: 			const newest = this.findNewestRegisteredSession();
633: 			if (newest) {
634: 				await this.bindSession(newest.id);
635: 			}
636: 		} catch (err) {
637: 			this.options.outputChannel?.appendLine(
638: 				`[AgentChatView] startNew failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
639: 			);
640: 			window.showErrorMessage(
641: 				`Failed to start agent chat: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
642: 			);
643: 		}
644: 	}
645: 
646: 	private findProvider(
647: 		id: string | undefined
648: 	): AgentChatCatalog["providers"][number] | undefined {
649: 		if (!id) {
650: 			return;
651: 		}
652: 		return this.snapshotCatalog().providers.find((p) => p.id === id);
653: 	}
654: 
655: 	private findNewestRegisteredSession(): AgentChatSession | undefined {
656: 		const sessions = this.options.registry.listActive();
657: 		if (sessions.length === 0) {
658: 			return;
659: 		}
660: 		return sessions.slice().sort((a, b) => b.createdAt - a.createdAt)[0];
661: 	}
662: 
663: 	private snapshotCatalog(): AgentChatCatalog {
664: 		const baseCatalog = buildAgentChatCatalog({
665: 			...this.options.catalogSources,
666: 			probeCache: this.probeCache,
667: 		});
668: 		const discovery = this.options.modelDiscovery;
669: 		if (!discovery) {
670: 			return baseCatalog;
671: 		}
672: 		const projectedProviders = baseCatalog.providers.map((provider) =>
673: 			this.applyDiscoveredModels(provider, discovery.peek(provider.id))
674: 		);
675: 		return { ...baseCatalog, providers: projectedProviders };
676: 	}
677: 
678: 	/**
679: 	 * Overlay a {@link DiscoveredModels} cache hit on top of a static
680: 	 * catalog projection. When the source is `"vscode-lm"` or
681: 	 * `"agent"` we replace the static models entirely; `"catalog"` and
682: 	 * `"none"` defer to the catalog snapshot the picker already had.
683: 	 */
684: 	private applyDiscoveredModels(
685: 		provider: AgentChatProviderOption,
686: 		discovered: DiscoveredModels | undefined
687: 	): AgentChatProviderOption {
688: 		if (!discovered) {
689: 			return provider;
690: 		}
691: 		if (discovered.source === "vscode-lm" || discovered.source === "agent") {
692: 			return { ...provider, models: discovered.models as ModelDescriptor[] };
693: 		}
694: 		if (discovered.source === "none") {
695: 			// Honour the redesign: when no source has any models, the
696: 			// picker hides the `<select>` entirely. We pass an empty
697: 			// list so the webview can detect the empty state.
698: 			return { ...provider, models: [] };
699: 		}
700: 		// `catalog` — keep the static list the base projector already used.
701: 		return provider;
702: 	}
703: 
704: 	/**
705: 	 * Probes every registered ACP provider in parallel and stores the
706: 	 * `installed` flag in {@link probeCache}. When at least one entry
707: 	 * changes the catalog is rebroadcast so the picker reflects reality.
708: 	 *
709: 	 * Re-entrant: concurrent calls share the same in-flight promise so
710: 	 * fast-firing events (registry update + tree click + new chat) do
711: 	 * not spawn multiple probe storms.
712: 	 */
713: 	private scheduleProbeRefresh(): void {
714: 		if (this.probeRefreshInFlight) {
715: 			return;
716: 		}
717: 		this.probeRefreshInFlight = this.refreshProbes()
718: 			.catch((err: unknown) => {
719: 				this.options.outputChannel?.appendLine(
720: 					`[AgentChatView] probe refresh failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
721: 				);
722: 			})
723: 			.finally(() => {
724: 				this.probeRefreshInFlight = undefined;
725: 			});
726: 	}
727: 
728: 	private async refreshProbes(): Promise<void> {
729: 		const registry = this.options.catalogSources.acpProviderRegistry;
730: 		if (!registry) {
731: 			return;
732: 		}
733: 		const descriptors = registry.list();
734: 		const results = await Promise.allSettled(
735: 			descriptors.map(async (descriptor) => {
736: 				const probe = await descriptor.probe();
737: 				return { id: descriptor.id, installed: probe.installed };
738: 			})
739: 		);
740: 		let mutated = false;
741: 		for (const outcome of results) {
742: 			if (outcome.status !== "fulfilled") {
743: 				continue;
744: 			}
745: 			const { id, installed } = outcome.value;
746: 			const previous = this.probeCache.get(id);
747: 			if (!previous || previous.installed !== installed) {
748: 				this.probeCache.set(id, { installed });
749: 				mutated = true;
750: 			}
751: 		}
752: 		if (mutated && this.view) {
753: 			const catalog = buildAgentChatCatalog({
754: 				...this.options.catalogSources,
755: 				probeCache: this.probeCache,
756: 			});
757: 			await this.postMessage({
758: 				type: "agent-chat/catalog/loaded",
759: 				payload: { catalog },
760: 			}).catch(noop);
761: 		}
762: 	}
763: 
764: 	private snapshotSessionList(): SidebarSessionListItem[] {
765: 		const all = [
766: 			...this.options.registry.listActive(),
767: 			...this.options.registry.listRecent(),
768: 		];
769: 		const seen = new Set<string>();
770: 		const items: SidebarSessionListItem[] = [];
771: 		for (const session of all) {
772: 			if (seen.has(session.id)) {
773: 				continue;
774: 			}
775: 			seen.add(session.id);
776: 			items.push({
777: 				id: session.id,
778: 				agentDisplayName: session.agentDisplayName,
779: 				lifecycleState: session.lifecycleState,
780: 				updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
781: 				selectedModeId: session.selectedModeId,
782: 				selectedModelId: session.selectedModelId,
783: 				isTerminal: TERMINAL_STATES.has(session.lifecycleState),
784: 				title: this.deriveSessionTitle(session.id),
785: 			});
786: 		}
787: 		items.sort((a, b) => b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);
788: 		return items;
789: 	}
790: 
791: 	/**
792: 	 * Look up the first {@link UserChatMessage} of a session and return a
793: 	 * truncated label suitable for the sidebar history list. Returns
794: 	 * `undefined` when no user message has been recorded yet.
795: 	 */
796: 	private deriveSessionTitle(sessionId: string): string | undefined {
797: 		const transcript = this.readTranscriptForSession(sessionId);
798: 		for (const message of transcript) {
799: 			if (message.role === "user" && typeof message.content === "string") {
800: 				const text = message.content.trim().replace(/\s+/g, " ");
801: 				if (text.length === 0) {
802: 					continue;
803: 				}
804: 				return text.length > SESSION_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH
805: 					? `${text.slice(0, SESSION_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH - 1).trimEnd()}\u2026`
806: 					: text;
807: 			}
808: 		}
809: 		return;
810: 	}
811: 
812: 	/**
813: 	 * Read the persisted transcript for `sessionId` straight from the
814: 	 * store's workspaceState. Mirrors {@link SidebarSessionBinding.readTranscript}
815: 	 * but lives at the provider level so {@link snapshotSessionList} can
816: 	 * peek at sessions that are not bound to the active webview.
817: 	 */
818: 	private readTranscriptForSession(sessionId: string): ChatMessage[] {
819: 		const key = transcriptKeyFor(sessionId);
820: 		const mem = (
821: 			this.options.store as unknown as {
822: 				workspaceState?: { get<T>(k: string): T | undefined };
823: 			}
824: 		).workspaceState;
825: 		if (!mem) {
826: 			return [];
827: 		}
828: 		const raw = mem.get<{ messages: ChatMessage[] }>(key);
829: 		return raw?.messages ?? [];
830: 	}
831: 
832: 	private async postMessage(message: unknown): Promise<void> {
833: 		if (!this.view) {
834: 			return;
835: 		}
836: 		try {
837: 			await this.view.webview.postMessage(message);
838: 		} catch (err) {
839: 			this.options.outputChannel?.appendLine(
840: 				`[AgentChatView] postMessage failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
841: 			);
842: 		}
843: 	}
844: }

846: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
847: // Inner: per-session binding (mirrors AgentChatPanel's session-bound subset)
848: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
849: 
850: interface SidebarSessionBindingOptions {
851: 	readonly session: AgentChatSession;
852: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
853: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
854: 	readonly postMessage: (message: unknown) => Promise<void>;
855: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
856: }
857: 
858: class SidebarSessionBinding {
859: 	readonly sessionId: string;
860: 	private readonly session: AgentChatSession;
861: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
862: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
863: 	private readonly postMessage: (message: unknown) => Promise<void>;
864: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
865: 	private readonly subscriptions: Disposable[] = [];
866: 	private readonly knownMessageIds = new Set<string>();
867: 	private lastLifecycleState: SessionLifecycleState;
868: 	private lastAvailableModelIds: string[] = [];
869: 	private lastCurrentModelId: string | undefined;
870: 	private disposed = false;
871: 
872: 	constructor(options: SidebarSessionBindingOptions) {
873: 		this.session = options.session;
874: 		this.sessionId = options.session.id;
875: 		this.store = options.store;
876: 		this.registry = options.registry;
877: 		this.postMessage = options.postMessage;
878: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
879: 		this.lastLifecycleState = options.session.lifecycleState;
880: 		this.lastAvailableModelIds = (options.session.availableModels ?? []).map(
881: 			(m) => m.id
882: 		);
883: 		this.lastCurrentModelId =
884: 			options.session.currentModelId ?? options.session.selectedModelId;
885: 
886: 		this.subscriptions.push(
887: 			this.store.onDidChangeManifest(() => {
888: 				this.onManifestChanged().catch((err) => {
889: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
890: 						`[AgentChatView] manifest sync failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`
891: 					);
892: 				});
893: 			})
894: 		);
895: 
896: 		// Phase 4 — wire the runner's pending-writes feed (if it
897: 		// exposes one) to the webview so the Cursor-style Accept/Reject
898: 		// bar can mirror the in-flight `writeTextFile` queue.
899: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.sessionId) as
900: 			| {
901: 					onPendingWritesChanged?: (
902: 						listener: (writes: readonly PendingWriteSnapshot[]) => void
903: 					) => Disposable;
904: 					onEvent?: (listener: (event: AgentChatEvent) => void) => Disposable;
905: 			  }
906: 			| undefined;
907: 		const subscribe = runner?.onPendingWritesChanged;
908: 		if (subscribe) {
909: 			this.subscriptions.push(
910: 				subscribe.call(runner, (writes) => {

921: 	/**

923: 	 * the `session/models-changed` variant — the runner emits this when

925: 	 * webview consumes to refresh the model chip without a full

934: 		}>;

969: 		this.disposed = true;

1051: 	async handleWebviewMessage(message: {

1055: 		switch (message.type) {
1056: 			case "agent-chat/input/submit":

1066: 				await this.routeToRunner("cancel");
1067: 				return;
1068: 			case "agent-chat/control/retry":
1069: 				await this.routeToRunner("retry");

1071: 			case "agent-chat/pending-writes/accept-all":
1072: 				this.flushPendingWrites({ kind: "accept-all" });
1073: 				return;
1074: 			case "agent-chat/pending-writes/reject-all":
1075: 				this.flushPendingWrites({ kind: "reject-all" });
1076: 				return;
1077: 			case "agent-chat/pending-writes/accept-one":
1078: 				this.flushPendingWrites({
1079: 					kind: "accept-one",
1080: 					id: (message.payload as { id?: string } | undefined)?.id ?? "",
1081: 				});
1082: 				return;
1083: 			case "agent-chat/pending-writes/reject-one":
1084: 				this.flushPendingWrites({
1085: 					kind: "reject-one",
1086: 					id: (message.payload as { id?: string } | undefined)?.id ?? "",
1087: 				});

1089: 			default:

1091: 		}
1092: 	}

1094: 	/**

1096: 	 * the manager reference and translates the action into a flush on
1097: 	 * the underlying ACP client's pending-writes store.
1098: 	 */
1099: 	private flushPendingWrites(action: {
1100: 		kind: "accept-all" | "reject-all" | "accept-one" | "reject-one";
1101: 		id?: string;
1102: 	}): void {
1103: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.sessionId) as
1104: 			| {
1105: 					flushPendingWrites?: (a: typeof action) => void;
1106: 			  }
1107: 			| undefined;

1111: 	private async handleInputSubmit(payload: {
1112: 		sessionId: string;
1113: 		content: string;
1114: 		clientMessageId?: string;
1115: 	}): Promise<void> {
1116: 		if (payload.sessionId !== this.sessionId) {
1117: 			return;
1118: 		}
1119: 		const current =
1120: 			(await this.store.getSession(this.sessionId)) ?? this.session;
1121: 		const userMessageId = payload.clientMessageId ?? randomUUID();
1122: 		const pendingMessage: UserChatMessage = {
1123: 			id: userMessageId,
1124: 			sessionId: this.sessionId,
1125: 			timestamp: Date.now(),
1126: 			sequence: 0,
1127: 			role: "user",
1128: 			content: payload.content,
1129: 			isInitialPrompt: false,
1130: 			deliveryStatus: "pending",
1131: 		};

1133: 		if (current.source === "cloud") {
1134: 			await this.emitMessage(pendingMessage, {
1135: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
1136: 				rejectionReason:

1140: 		}
1141: 
1142: 		if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(current.lifecycleState)) {

1146: 			});

1148: 		}
1149: 
1150: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.sessionId);
1151: 		if (!runner) {
1152: 			await this.emitMessage(pendingMessage, {
1153: 				deliveryStatus: "rejected",
1154: 				rejectionReason: "No active runner attached to this session.",
1155: 			});

1159: 		this.knownMessageIds.add(pendingMessage.id);
1160: 
1161: 		if (!runner.submit) {
1162: 			await this.sendMessagesUpdated([
1163: 				{

1166: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",
1167: 						rejectionReason: "This runner does not accept follow-up input.",
1168: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,

1170: 			]);

1173: 		try {
1174: 			await runner.submit(payload.content);

1176: 				{

1185: 					patch: {
1186: 						deliveryStatus: "rejected",

1188: 					} as Partial<ChatMessage>,

1190: 			]);

1192: 	}

1194: 	private async routeToRunner(method: "cancel" | "retry"): Promise<void> {
1195: 		const runner = this.registry.getRunner(this.sessionId);
1196: 		if (!runner) {
1197: 			return;
1198: 		}
1199: 		try {
1200: 			if (method === "cancel") {
1201: 				await runner.cancel();
1202: 			} else if (runner.retry) {
1203: 				await runner.retry();
1204: 			}
1205: 		} catch (err) {
1206: 			logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.ERROR, {
1207: 				sessionId: this.sessionId,

1209: 				error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),

1211: 			});

1215: 	private async onManifestChanged(): Promise<void> {
1216: 		const current = await this.store.getSession(this.sessionId);
1217: 		if (!current) {

1220: 		if (current.lifecycleState !== this.lastLifecycleState) {
1221: 			const from = this.lastLifecycleState;
1222: 			this.lastLifecycleState = current.lifecycleState;
1223: 			await this.postMessage({
1224: 				type: "agent-chat/session/lifecycle-changed",
1225: 				payload: {

1227: 					from,
1228: 					to: current.lifecycleState,
1229: 					at: Date.now(),

1237: 	/**

1240: 	 * `agent-chat/session/models-changed`. The runner persists the new

1244: 	private async maybePushModelsChanged(
1245: 		current: AgentChatSession

1248: 		const nextCurrent = current.currentModelId ?? current.selectedModelId;

1250: 			nextIds.length !== this.lastAvailableModelIds.length ||
1251: 			nextIds.some((id, idx) => this.lastAvailableModelIds[idx] !== id);

1263: 
1264: 	private async flushTranscriptDeltas(): Promise<void> {
1265: 		const transcript = this.readTranscript(this.sessionId);

1278: 	private async emitMessage(

1352: 		case "worktree":

1371: function composePromptWithAgentFile(

1373: 	agentFileId: string | undefined,

1376: 	if (!agentFileId) {

1379: 	const match = agentFiles.find((entry) => entry.id === agentFileId);
1380: 	if (!match) {

1383: 	const reference = match.absolutePath
1384: 		? `[agent-file: ${match.displayName} — ${match.absolutePath}]`

1386: 	return `${reference}\n\n${prompt}`;

1400: 		.get<string>("acp.permissionDefault", "ask");
1401: 	if (raw === "allow" || raw === "deny") {

/**
 * AgentChatViewProvider — the canonical Agent Chat sidebar webview view.
 *
 * Lives inside the shared `gatomia` activity-bar container (alongside Specs,
 * Hooks, Steering, etc.) and owns a single `vscode.WebviewView`. On hosts
 * that support it, the view is migrated individually to the auxiliary side
 * bar — see `agent-chat-entry-points.ts` for the migration logic.
 *
 * The view can show three states:
 *
 *   1. **Empty / idle** — no session bound. The webview composer doubles as a
 *      "new session" launcher: the user picks a provider, model, and agent
 *      file from the catalog dropdowns and submits an initial prompt. The
 *      view dispatches `gatomia.agentChat.startNew` and binds to the
 *      newly-created session as soon as the registry attaches it.
 *
 *   2. **Active session** — a session is bound. The view forwards transcript
 *      mutations from `AgentChatSessionStore` and lifecycle changes from the
 *      registry; the composer becomes a follow-up input bar.
 *

59: 	implements TreeDataProvider<CloudAgentTreeItem>
60: {
61: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
62: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage | undefined;
63: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
64: 		CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null
65: 	>();
66: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null> =
67: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
68: 
69: 	constructor(
70: 		registry: ProviderRegistry,
71: 		sessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage
72: 	) {
73: 		this.registry = registry;
74: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	/**
78: 	 * Refresh the tree view.
79: 	 */
80: 	refresh(): void {
81: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(null);
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/**
85: 	 * Update the context key for welcome content visibility.
86: 	 */
87: 	async updateContextKeys(): Promise<void> {
88: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
89: 		await commands.executeCommand(
90: 			"setContext",
91: 			"gatomia.cloudAgent.hasProvider",
92: 			active !== undefined
93: 		);
94: 		if (active) {
95: 			await commands.executeCommand(
96: 				"setContext",
97: 				"gatomia.cloudAgent.activeProvider",
98: 				active.metadata.id
99: 			);
100: 		}
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	getTreeItem(element: CloudAgentTreeItem): CloudAgentTreeItem {
104: 		return element;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	async getChildren(
108: 		element?: CloudAgentTreeItem
109: 	): Promise<CloudAgentTreeItem[]> {
110: 		if (element?.session && !element.agentTask) {
111: 			return this.getSessionChildren(element.session);
112: 		}
113: 
114: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
115: 		if (!active) {
116: 			return [];
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		if (!this.sessionStorage) {
120: 			return [];
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		const allSessions = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
124: 		if (allSessions.length === 0) {
125: 			const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
126: 				"No sessions",
127: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
128: 			);
129: 			item.description = active.metadata.displayName;
130: 			return [item];
131: 		}
132: 
133: 		return allSessions.map((s) =>
134: 			this.createSessionItem(s, active.metadata.id)
135: 		);
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	private createSessionItem(
139: 		session: AgentSession,
140: 		activeProviderId: string
141: 	): CloudAgentTreeItem {
142: 		const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
143: 		const label = firstTask
144: 			? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
145: 			: session.localId;
146: 
147: 		const hasTasks =
148: 			session.tasks.length > 0 || session.pullRequests.length > 0;
149: 		const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
150: 			label,
151: 			hasTasks
152: 				? TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed
153: 				: TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
154: 			session
155: 		);
156: 
157: 		item.description = session.isReadOnly
158: 			? `${session.status} (read-only)`
159: 			: session.status;
160: 
161: 		const tooltipLines = [
162: 			`Status: ${session.status}`,
163: 			`Provider: ${session.providerId}`,
164: 			`Branch: ${session.branch}`,
165: 			`Spec: ${session.specPath}`,
166: 		];
167: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
168: 			tooltipLines.push(`URL: ${session.externalUrl}`);
169: 		}
170: 		if (session.tasks.length > 1) {
171: 			tooltipLines.push(`Tasks: ${session.tasks.length}`);
172: 		}
173: 		if (session.pullRequests.length > 0) {
174: 			tooltipLines.push(`PRs: ${session.pullRequests.length}`);
175: 		}
176: 		item.tooltip = tooltipLines.join("\n");
177: 
178: 		item.iconPath = this.getStatusIcon(session.status);
179: 		item.contextValue =
180: 			session.providerId === activeProviderId && !session.isReadOnly
181: 				? `session.${session.status}`
182: 				: "session.readonly";
183: 
184: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
185: 			item.command = {
186: 				command: "vscode.open",
187: 				title: "Open in Browser",
188: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
189: 			};
190: 		}
191: 
192: 		return item;
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	private getSessionChildren(session: AgentSession): CloudAgentTreeItem[] {
196: 		const children: CloudAgentTreeItem[] = [];
197: 
198: 		for (const task of session.tasks) {
199: 			const taskItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
200: 				`${task.specTaskId}: ${task.title}`,
201: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
202: 				session,
203: 				task
204: 			);
205: 			taskItem.description = task.status;
206: 			taskItem.iconPath = this.getTaskStatusIcon(task.status);
207: 			taskItem.tooltip = [
208: 				`Task: ${task.title}`,
209: 				`Status: ${task.status}`,
210: 				`Priority: ${task.priority}`,
211: 			].join("\n");
212: 			children.push(taskItem);
213: 		}
214: 
215: 		for (const pr of session.pullRequests) {
216: 			const prItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
217: 				`PR: ${pr.branch}`,
218: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
219: 				session
220: 			);
221: 			prItem.description = pr.state ?? "open";
222: 			prItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-pull-request");
223: 			prItem.tooltip = pr.url;
224: 			prItem.command = {
225: 				command: "vscode.open",
226: 				title: "Open Pull Request",
227: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(pr.url)],
228: 			};
229: 			children.push(prItem);
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
233: 			const linkItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
234: 				"Open in Provider",
235: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
236: 				session
237: 			);
238: 			linkItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("link-external");
239: 			linkItem.tooltip = session.externalUrl;
240: 			linkItem.command = {
241: 				command: "vscode.open",
242: 				title: "Open in Browser",
243: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
244: 			};
245: 			children.push(linkItem);
246: 		}
247: 
248: 		return children;
249: 	}
250: 
251: 	private getStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
252: 		switch (status) {
253: 			case "running":
254: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
255: 			case "completed":
256: 				return new ThemeIcon("check");
257: 			case "failed":
258: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
259: 			case "blocked":
260: 				return new ThemeIcon("warning");
261: 			case "cancelled":
262: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
263: 			default:
264: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
265: 		}
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	private getTaskStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
269: 		switch (status) {
270: 			case "in_progress":
271: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
272: 			case "completed":
273: 				return new ThemeIcon("pass");
274: 			case "failed":
275: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
276: 			case "skipped":
277: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
278: 			default:
279: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
280: 		}
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	dispose(): void {
284: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
285: 	}
286: }

1: /**

3:  *
4:  * Responsibilities (Phase 3 / US1 MVP):
5:  *   - Manage a single `vscode.WebviewPanel` instance per session (FR-008).
6:  *   - Load the `agent-chat` webview entry via the shared
7:  *     `getWebviewContent()` helper (registered as a SupportedPage in T004).
8:  *   - Handle the `agent-chat/*` protocol messages relevant to User Story 1:
9:  *     hydration (`ready` → `session/loaded`), input submission with
10:  *     `deliveryStatus` lifecycle, cancel / retry routing.
11:  *   - Forward `AgentChatSessionStore` mutations (manifest + transcript) and
12:  *     registry events to the webview as `messages/appended`,
13:  *     `messages/updated`, `session/lifecycle-changed`.
14:  *
15:  * Out of scope for US1 (deferred to later phases):
16:  *   - worktree banner + cleanup flow (T066, Phase 5)
17:  *   - mode / model / target selectors (T066, Phase 5)

51: // ============================================================================
52: // Host abstraction

55: /**

60: export interface AgentChatPanelHost {

89: export function createDefaultAgentChatPanelHost(

116: function wrapVscodePanel(panel: WebviewPanel): HostedPanel {

156: export class AgentChatPanel {
157: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.agentChatPanel";
158: 
159: 	private readonly session: AgentChatSession;
160: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
161: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
162: 	private readonly host: AgentChatPanelHost;
163: 
164: 	private panel: HostedPanel | undefined;
165: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
166: 	private opened = false;
167: 	private lastLifecycleState: SessionLifecycleState;
168: 
169: 	/**
170: 	 * Fired exactly once when this `AgentChatPanel` is disposed. Used by
171: 	 * the host wrapper exposed to the registry so the registry can clean
172: 	 * up its `panelsBySessionId` entry without coupling to the inner
173: 	 * `WebviewPanel`.
174: 	 */
175: 	private readonly _onDidDispose = new EventEmitter<void>();
176: 	readonly onDidDispose = this._onDidDispose.event;
177: 
178: 	constructor(options: AgentChatPanelOptions) {
179: 		this.session = options.session;
180: 		this.store = options.store;
181: 		this.registry = options.registry;
182: 		this.host = options.host;
183: 		this.lastLifecycleState = options.session.lifecycleState;
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
187: 	// Lifecycle
188: 	// ------------------------------------------------------------------
189: 
190: 	/**
191: 	 * Create (or reveal) the webview panel, wire message plumbing, and
192: 	 * subscribe to store/registry updates. Idempotent — subsequent calls
193: 	 * focus the existing panel.
194: 	 */
195: 	open(): void {
196: 		if (this.opened && this.panel) {
197: 			this.panel.reveal(ViewColumn.Beside);
198: 			return;
199: 		}
200: 		this.panel = this.host.createPanel({ session: this.session });
201: 		this.opened = true;
202: 
203: 		// NOTE: We intentionally do NOT call `this.registry.attachPanel(...)`
204: 		// here. The owning command handler (`handleStartNew` /
205: 		// `handleOpenForSession`) is the single source of truth for panel
206: 		// registration — it attaches a wrapper that exposes our
207: 		// `onDidDispose` event so the registry can clean up. Calling
208: 		// `attachPanel` again from this site would violate the registry's
209: 		// "one panel per session" invariant and cause initialization to
210: 		// fail (regression seen in spec 018: "session ... already has a
211: 		// panel" thrown right after the user submitted the new-session
212: 		// prompt).
